title: Optimistic Mutation Lifecycle (apply / reconcile / rollback)
summary: Surveys the apply-then-reconcile-then-rollback model every server-state library converged on — TanStack Query useMutation (onMutate / onError / onSettled + invalidateQueries), Apollo optimisticResponse + cache update, SWR useSWRMutation (optimisticData / rollbackOnError), RTK Query updateQueryData + patchResult.undo(), Relay optimisticUpdater — and grounds the placement decision #1395. Key finding: WE already ships this lifecycle as the resource-action block (withOptimisticMutation trait, resource-action-revert event, previousState snapshot), so the open question is contract placement, not a build. The mutation lifecycle has no owning intent (it squats on loader.strategy.optimistic, a read-side dimension), and its rollback half is the same reversibility primitive — the change-record semantic's oldValue, carried because RFC 6902 ops are not self-inverting — that undo/redo (#1394) needs. Recommends a first-class mutation intent (symmetric to loader for reads) and aligning the rollback contract on change-record / the Change Tracking protocol.
